title: Understanding Conservation of Momentum
text: In a collision where two objects stick together, the total momentum before the collision is equal to the total momentum after the collision. This is due to the conservation of momentum principle.

title: Calculate Initial Momentum
text: Calculate the initial momentum of the bullet before the collision. Use the formula: momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity.

title: Initial Momentum of Bullet
text: What is the initial momentum of the bullet? (Use mass $$=$$ $$0.2$$ kg and velocity $$=$$ $$400$$ m/s)

title: Calculate Total Mass After Collision
text: After the collision, the bullet and block move together as one unit. Calculate the total mass of this combination.

title: Total Mass of Block and Bullet
text: What is the total mass of the block and bullet combination? (Use mass of block $$=$$ $$1.5$$ kg and mass of bullet $$=$$ $$0.2$$ kg)

title: Calculate Final Velocity
text: Use the conservation of momentum to find the final velocity of the $$\frac{block}{bullet}$$ combination. The formula is: (initial momentum of bullet) $$=$$ (total mass) * (final velocity).

title: Final Velocity Calculation
text: Solve for the final velocity using the equation: $$80$$ $$=$$ $$1.7$$ * velocity.

title: Direction of Velocity
text: Since the bullet was initially moving towards the east, the direction of the velocity of the $$\frac{block}{bullet}$$ combination after the collision will also be towards the east.
